sections.heading: Welcome
sections.content: Welcome to the first step of your journey into Android development! Before we write any code, it’s important to understand why Kotlin has become the gold standard for modern application development.

sections.heading: What is Kotlin?
sections.content: Kotlin is a cross-platform, statically typed, general-purpose programming language developed by JetBrains. It was officially announced by Google as a first-class language for Android development in 2017.

sections.heading: Key Characteristics
items: Interoperable: You can use Java and Kotlin in the same project side-by-side.
Concise: Kotlin reduces boilerplate code compared to Java.

sections.heading: Why Kotlin for Android?
items: Modern Syntax with properties, lambdas, and extension functions.

sections.heading: Your First Program: Hello World
sections.content: In Kotlin, the entry point of any program is the main function.

sections.heading: Hello World Breakdown
items: fun: Keyword used to declare a function.
main: Entry point of the program.
println(): Prints output to the console.

sections.heading: Environment Setup
items: IDE: IntelliJ IDEA or Android Studio.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Kotlin is preferred for Android development.
It is concise, safe, and interoperable with Java.
The main function is the starting point of every Kotlin program.
